title: How to Install NVIDIA Drivers on Linux
description: Install the correct NVIDIA proprietary drivers on Ubuntu and other Linux distributions for gaming, CUDA, and general performance.

steps.title: Check your NVIDIA GPU model
steps.content: Before installing drivers, identify your exact GPU model. This determines which driver version you need.

steps.title: See recommended drivers
steps.content: Ubuntu can detect your GPU and recommend the best driver. The `ubuntu-drivers` tool lists all compatible drivers and marks the recommended one.

steps.title: Install the recommended driver automatically
steps.content: The easiest method — let Ubuntu pick and install the best driver for your card. This works on Ubuntu, Pop!_OS, Linux Mint, and other Ubuntu-based distros.

steps.title: Or install a specific driver version
steps.content: If you need a specific version (for example, for CUDA compatibility or a newer GPU), install it directly with APT.
steps.code:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install nvidia-driver-560
steps.warning: Don't install drivers from NVIDIA's website (.run file) unless you know what you're doing. It can break your system on kernel updates.

steps.title: Reboot your system
steps.content: NVIDIA drivers require a reboot to load. The new driver replaces the open-source `nouveau` driver.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.content: After rebooting, check that the NVIDIA driver is active. `nvidia-smi` shows your GPU info, driver version, and current usage.

steps.title: Open NVIDIA Settings
steps.content: The NVIDIA Settings panel lets you configure resolution, multiple monitors, performance modes, and more. You can also launch it from your application menu.

seo.title: How to Install NVIDIA Drivers on Linux (Ubuntu, Mint, Pop!_OS)
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to installing NVIDIA proprietary drivers on Linux. Covers automatic installation, manual setup, and verification.
